beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and coffee at home while listening to the rain.
beats.reasoning: Low energy (40/100) and grey drizzly weather calls for a slower morning ritual. Homebody tendency means she'll linger over coffee rather than rush. Sets tone for a quieter Tuesday.

beats.activity: Commute to Renton workplace via I-405 south, avoiding SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; she departs early (before 9am) and takes I-405 south to avoid the snarl. Cybersecurity consultant has regular Renton office days. Budget generous time for rain-slicked roads.

beats.activity: Cybersecurity audit work and client calls in Renton office.
beats.reasoning: Consultant work is steady and methodical in morning hours. Rain outside keeps her focused indoors. Yesterday's energy was high from World Cup outing; today's low energy and drizzle suggest she'll stay put at desk rather than venture out for lunch.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at desk or nearby café in Renton; rain persists.
beats.reasoning: Rain still falling at noon (ends ~2pm). Homebody personality and low energy make her stay local rather than drive across town. Quick meal to fuel afternoon work.

beats.activity: Continue client work and infrastructure reviews.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block. Rain clears by 2pm but energy remains low; she's productive but not pushing hard. No urge to venture out like yesterday's World Cup detour—content to stay focused on work.

beats.activity: Drive home to Bellevue via I-405 north; rain has cleared.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure ended at noon; I-405 is now clear. Evening drive home as rain clears. Consultant travels often, so commute is routine. Budget 50min for evening traffic even though rain has cleared.

beats.activity: Prepare simple dinner and eat at home.
beats.reasoning: Homebody who values quiet evenings. Low energy and a full day at the office make her opt for home cooking rather than dining out. Still recovering from yesterday's high social energy.

beats.activity: Work on pottery wheel; throw and trim a new bowl.; Wind down with tea, review film negatives and journal notes from yesterday's waterfront walk.
beats.reasoning: Pottery is her free-time passion. Low energy and grey weather make hands-on creative work restorative. She mentioned yesterday that her hands needed clay after driving; today's quiet evening is perfect for studio time.
Full overnight rest after a steady, introspective day. 7h 45min sleep allows recovery from low energy. Ends before 06:30 next morning as required.

diary:
Woke to that steady patter on the windows—the kind of rain that makes you want to linger over coffee. I-405 was my route south; everyone else was probably cursing the SR-520 closure, but I'd already mapped around it. Renton office was quiet, just me and the client calls. The audit work felt routine, almost meditative after years of the same patterns.

Lunch at my desk, rain still drumming outside. By afternoon the sky had cleared enough that the drive back felt lighter somehow. I made something simple—just pasta—and then I was at the wheel. There's something about centering clay that untangles the day. My hands remembered the motion, the resistance, the way it all comes together. Later, tea and those negatives from yesterday's walk. The waterfront looked different in black and white.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure were background noise for me—I've been doing this commute long enough to anticipate disruptions. I chose I-405 automatically, didn't even register it as a choice. The work was solid but didn't demand much of me emotionally. I think I was saving energy for the evening, for the pottery. That's become my real anchor lately, more than the consulting gigs. The negatives pulled me back to yesterday's walk, which means the waterfront is still with me. I'm in one of those phases where I need to make things with my hands more than I need to solve problems on screens.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
